title: write_smt2: Error when using "-nobv" option
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.22+57 (git sha1 518194fac, clang 11.0.1-2 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

It seems like the `-nobv` option with `wirte_smt2` is not working properly.
My module:
```
// simple 3 way round-robin arbiter
module top (
    input            clk,
    input            rstn,
    input      [2:0] req,
    output     [2:0] grant
);

    reg [2:0] p;
    wire [5:0] double_req;
    wire [5:0] double_gnt;

    always @(posedge clk or negedge rstn) begin
        if (!rstn)
        begin
            p <= 3'b001;
        end
        else
        begin
            p <= {p[1:0],p[2]};
        end
    end

    assign double_req = {req,req};
    assign double_gnt = double_req & ~(double_req - p);
    assign grant = double_gnt[5:3] | double_gnt[2:0];

endmodule
```
Yosys commands:
```
read_verilog top.v
prep
async2sync
dffunmap
write_smt2 -nobv
```

### Expected Behavior

Writing out smt-lib format files without error.

### Actual Behavior

Yosys reports:
```
ERROR: Unsupported cell type $dff for cell arbiter.$procdff$7.
```

Note: 
`write_smt2` and `write_smt2 -nomem` is working.
